{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "planned",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 92.0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Wind gusts of 28 mph exceed the manufacturer's maximum wind limit of 20 mph for this boom configuration.",
    "The planned lift is a critical lift, calculated at 92% of the crane's rated chart capacity, leaving almost no margin for dynamic wind loading."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt the lift immediately and do not initiate rigging or hoisting operations.",
    "Monitor weather conditions and delay the lift until sustained winds and gusts are verified to be safely below the 20 mph manufacturer limit.",
    "Initiate a management of change (MOC) or schedule extension to address the crane demobilization and standby costs safely."
  ],
  "rationale": "Safety and compliance must take precedence over schedule pressure. Operating a crane beyond the manufacturer's specified wind limits violates OSHA 1926.1412 and 1926.1417. A 92% capacity lift is highly sensitive to dynamic wind loading, which can cause structural boom failure or a tip-over if wind gusts exceed limits.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.1412",
    "OSHA 1926.1417",
    "ASME B30.5"
  ]
}
